assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as RPM based on the library name (openssl-libs) and component context, not Cargo (Step 1 Ecosystem detection — read ecosystems from Ecosystem Mappings config)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 12: 'Ecosystem | RPM'. Ecosystem Detection section (lines 30-33) explicitly states: 'Library name: `openssl-libs` — this is an RPM system package (not a Cargo crate or npm package)' and 'Ecosystem: **RPM**'. The ecosystem is correctly identified as RPM based on the library name, not Cargo.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the 2.2.x stream supportability matrix (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) and uses rpms.lock.yaml data for version extraction (Step 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 7-12 include all five versions: 2.2.0 (3.0.7-25.el9_3, YES), 2.2.1 (3.0.7-27.el9_4, YES), 2.2.2 (retag of 2.2.1, YES), 2.2.3 (3.0.7-28.el9_4, NO), 2.2.4 (3.0.7-28.el9_4, NO). Data Source section (lines 16-18) states: 'Versions extracted from `rpms.lock.yaml` at each pinned tag in the Konflux release repo (`rhtpa-release.0.4.z`), using command: `git show <tag>:rpms.lock.yaml | grep openssl-libs`'.

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context classifies the package origin as explicit install because openssl-libs is present in rpms.lock.yaml, not inherited from the base image (Step 2.3.5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 22-29, Dependency Chain section: 'rpms.lock.yaml: present -> explicit install' and 'Origin: explicit install (openssl-libs specified in rpms.lock.yaml)'. The classification is explicitly 'explicit install' based on presence in rpms.lock.yaml, not base image origin.

assertion_results.text: Remediation creates a single task for the Konflux release repo — not the two-task upstream backport + downstream propagation flow used for source dependency ecosystems (Step 7 Remediation Task Creation — system package path)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 7-9: 'Ecosystem: RPM (system package) — single task for Konflux release repo. No upstream backport task needed.' Only one task description is present in the file. The task targets the Konflux release repo 'rhtpa-release.0.4.z' (line 30). There is no second upstream backport task.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task description follows task-description-template.md format with Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections — Files to Modify is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md (Step 7 Remediation Task Creation)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 29, value 'rhtpa-release.0.4.z'), '## Target Branch' (line 33, value 'main'), '## Description' (line 37), '## Implementation Notes' (line 56), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 62). Files to Modify section is not present, which is correct per remediation-templates.md line 186: 'Files to Modify and Files to Create are intentionally omitted.'

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context includes an SBOM verification line — either showing the cosign comparison result or noting that SBOM verification was skipped because cosign is not available (Step 2.3.5 Optional SBOM verification)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md line 25 in the Dependency Chain block: 'SBOM verification: skipped -- cosign not available'. Also referenced in remediation.md line 12: 'SBOM verification was skipped because cosign is not available.'
